verified.name: Open Government, UK
verified.tagline: Query the UK's open-data catalogue over CKAN, no key needed
verified.description: data.gov.uk runs on CKAN, so you can hit its /api/3/action/ endpoints to search or list tens of thousands of published UK public-sector datasets. Responses are JSON and CORS is wide open. Individual dataset licences vary, though most sit under the Open Government Licence.
verified.dataLicense: Open Government Licence v3.0 (varies per dataset)
verified.notes: Standard CKAN API; per-dataset licences differ, but the OGL default permits commercial reuse.

verified.name: Open Government, Victoria State Government
verified.tagline: Victoria's public datasets, searchable via a CKAN JSON API
verified.description: The Victorian Government's open-data portal exposes a standard CKAN action API at discover.data.vic.gov.au. Ask it to list or search the state's datasets and you get clean JSON with permissive CORS. Most data defaults to CC BY 4.0 under the DataVic access policy.
verified.notes: Portal host is discover.data.vic.gov.au; DataVic access policy defaults datasets to CC BY 4.0.

verified.name: Open Government, West Australia
verified.tagline: Browse Western Australia's open data through CKAN endpoints
verified.description: Western Australia's data catalogue answers CKAN /api/3/action/ calls at catalogue.data.wa.gov.au, returning JSON lists and metadata for state datasets (heavy on geology and mapping). No key, no signup, CORS enabled. Licensing is per-dataset, so check before you reuse.
verified.notes: Portal host is catalogue.data.wa.gov.au; licences differ per dataset, so commercial reuse is not blanket-stated.

verified.name: Represent by Open North
verified.tagline: Map any Canadian postcode to its elected reps and districts
verified.description: Open North's Represent API turns a Canadian postal code or point into the electoral districts and representatives that cover it, across federal, provincial, and municipal levels. Add ?format=json for a tidy JSON response; CORS is open. It's free and keyless.
verified.notes: Append ?format=json to get JSON instead of the HTML API browser.

verified.name: USAspending.gov
verified.tagline: The firehose of US federal spending, straight as JSON
verified.description: USAspending is the official source for how the US government spends money, and its v2 REST API serves agencies, awards, and budget figures without a key. This toptier-agencies call is a simple GET, though many deeper queries expect POST bodies. The data is US federal public-domain.
verified.notes: Many endpoints require POST; this references GET is keyless and returns JSON.

verified.name: Covid Tracking Project
verified.tagline: The Covid Tracking Project's frozen US pandemic archive
verified.description: This is the archived v2 API from The Covid Tracking Project, serving daily US testing, hospitalization, and outcome figures as JSON. Data collection stopped on 7 March 2021, so treat it as a historical dataset rather than a live feed. It's licensed CC BY 4.0 with open CORS.
verified.notes: Historical archive: the project ended data collection on 2021-03-07 and no longer updates.

verified.name: Covid-19 India
verified.tagline: Static JSON snapshots of India's Covid-19 numbers by state
verified.description: data.covid19india.org publishes flat JSON files with state- and district-level case, recovery, and vaccination counts for India. There are no query parameters — you just fetch the file you want. The volunteer project wound down, so the data is frozen in late 2021.
verified.notes: Static data files with no query params; dataset is frozen — the project stopped updating around Nov 2021.

verified.name: Healthcare.gov
verified.tagline: Healthcare.gov's marketplace content, dumped as JSON
verified.description: Healthcare.gov exposes its editorial content — glossary terms, articles, and guidance about the US health-insurance marketplace — as static JSON via /api. It's built for reuse and translation, with each entry carrying both English and Spanish text. No key, US-government content.

sampleJson.title: Affordability Exemption
sampleJson.bite: An exemption that's needed when applying for Catastrophic coverage for people 30 years or older whose coverage is unaffordable.

verified.notes: Content/glossary API; entries include English and Spanish text.

verified.name: MedlinePlus Genetics
verified.tagline: Plain-language genetics reference from the NLM, in JSON
verified.description: The US National Library of Medicine serves its MedlinePlus Genetics articles — conditions, genes, chromosomes, and mtDNA — as downloadable JSON files, one per topic. Fetch a condition by name and you get its summary, synonyms, and linked resources. Heads up: the CORS header only allows *.nlm.nih.gov origins.

text.html: Alkaptonuria is an inherited condition that causes arthritis, kidney stones, spots of dark pigmentation, and dark urine...

verified.dataLicense: U.S. National Library of Medicine (see NLM terms)
verified.notes: access-control-allow-origin was present but restricted to *.nlm.nih.gov — not open to arbitrary browser origins.

verified.name: NPPES
verified.tagline: Look up any US healthcare provider by their NPI number
verified.description: The CMS NPPES registry API returns public records for US healthcare providers — names, addresses, and taxonomies — keyed by National Provider Identifier. Pass version=2.1 plus at least one search field (like state and city) and you get JSON back. No key, public data.
verified.notes: Requires version=2.1 and at least one search criterion; empty queries are rejected.

verified.name: Open Data NHS Scotland
verified.tagline: Public Health Scotland's open health data over CKAN
verified.description: Scotland's health open-data portal runs CKAN, so its /api/3/action/ endpoints list and search datasets on hospital activity, prescribing, demographics, and more. Responses are JSON, though this host returned no CORS header on our probe. Most datasets carry the Open Government Licence.
verified.notes: Standard CKAN API from Public Health Scotland; OGL default permits commercial reuse.

verified.name: Genrenator
verified.tagline: Invents a nonsense music genre on every request
verified.description: Genrenator is a delightfully useless toy: hit its endpoint and it hands back one freshly generated, faintly plausible music-genre name as a JSON string (think 'bubblegum hands up new age'). No key needed, and CORS echoes your origin. Perfect filler for demos and jokes.
verified.notes: Response body is a single generated JSON string; CORS echoed the request Origin.

verified.name: Lyrics.ovh
verified.tagline: Fetch a song's lyrics from just artist and title
verified.description: Lyrics.ovh takes an artist and song title in the URL path and returns the lyrics as a single JSON field. It's keyless with open CORS, which makes it a common first stop for lyric-fetching side projects. The lyrics themselves are third-party copyrighted text, so mind reuse.

sampleJson.lyrics:
Turn your magic on, umi, she'd say
Everything you want's a dream away
And we are legends every day
That's what she told me
...

verified.notes: Path is /v1/{artist}/{title}; returned lyrics text is copyrighted by third parties.

verified.name: MusicBrainz
verified.tagline: The open music encyclopedia's read API, JSON on request
verified.description: MusicBrainz is a community-maintained database of artists, releases, and recordings, and its web service lets you look entities up by MBID (append ?fmt=json for JSON). Play nice: it asks for roughly one request per second and a descriptive User-Agent. Core data is CC0.
verified.rateLimit: ~1 request/second per IP; descriptive User-Agent required
verified.dataLicense: Core data CC0; some supplementary data CC BY-NC-SA 3.0
verified.notes: Append &fmt=json for JSON; the 1 req/sec limit and User-Agent requirement are enforced.

verified.name: Openwhyd
verified.tagline: Curated streaming-track playlists as JSON feeds
verified.description: Openwhyd is an open-source music-curation platform; append ?format=json to its pages and you get JSON lists of user-posted tracks that link out to YouTube, SoundCloud, and friends. This /hot endpoint returns what's currently trending. Keyless, though no CORS header came back on our probe.

tracks.name: Manon Lisa - Le Petit Pêcheur
tracks.uNm: Médiathèques de la Communauté Urbaine d'Alençon

verified.notes: Append ?format=json; tracks are user-curated links out to YouTube/SoundCloud etc.

skipped.name: Open Government, USA
skipped.reason: catalog.data.gov CKAN action API returns a generic 404 (Not Found) for package_list and package_search across 3 attempts; no working keyless JSON GET could be verified.

skipped.name: Coronavirus
skipped.reason: Pipedream readme is a JS SPA that exposes no callable endpoint; a 2020-era personal workflow with no verifiable live keyless JSON GET.

skipped.name: Coronavirus in the UK
skipped.reason: Host api.coronavirus.data.gov.uk no longer resolves (DNS gone); the UK coronavirus dashboard API was decommissioned.

skipped.name: COVID-19 Tracker Canada
skipped.reason: api.covid19tracker.ca /summary and /provinces return HTTP 200 with an empty text/html body (no JSON), even with a browser User-Agent; API appears defunct.

skipped.name: Dataflow Kit COVID-19
skipped.reason: covid-19.dataflowkit.com/v1 enters an infinite redirect loop (301) and never returns JSON.

skipped.name: MyVaccination
skipped.reason: Postman documenter page is a JS SPA with no extractable base URL; no live keyless JSON endpoint could be determined (likely a defunct personal Heroku-era backend).

skipped.name: Deepcode
skipped.reason: deepcode.ai now serves Snyk marketing HTML after acquisition; no keyless public JSON API is offered.

skipped.name: Gaana
skipped.reason: GitHub project is self-host-only (run app.py / deploy your own); no official public keyless endpoint exists.

skipped.name: JioSaavn
skipped.reason: Project is self-host-only; the only reachable instance is an unofficial third-party Vercel scraper of a commercial service, not a reliable or official endpoint to list.
